title: Different versions of English Releases of End of Evangelion
display_title: Different versions of English Releases of End of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Different versions of English Releases of End of Evangelion?             Quote Post by futuramafreak » Sat Apr 21, 2012 10:16 pm I managed to find two copies of EoE today, so I bought both of them, but the spines are different. One is purple and one is red. There are no other differences I can see anywhere in the boxart or in the content of the discs. Same insert as well. They are definitely both authentic releases. I just wondered if Manga rereleased EoE at some point and changed the artwork slightly. Please let me know what you think. I can provide a picture if it's hard to believe. Thanks.

[]: Re: Different versions of English Releases of End of Evangel             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Apr 21, 2012 10:30 pm futuramafreak wrote: I managed to find two copies of EoE today, so I bought both of them, but the spines are different. One is purple and one is red. There are no other differences I can see anywhere in the boxart or in the content of the discs. Same insert as well. They are definitely both authentic releases. I just wondered if Manga rereleased EoE at some point and changed the artwork slightly. Please let me know what you think. I can provide a picture if it's hard to believe. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by futuramafreak » Sun Apr 22, 2012 1:24 am Here is a picture: [url]http://img845.imageshack.us/img845/8370/img3306v.jpg[/url] I realized looking at this picture, comparing the Manga logos, that the purple one might just be completely washed out of red. It's a pretty drastic change, though. I just might not have seen that before because the front and back covers are identical. D&R and the first EoE came from the same place, and you can kinda see the washing-out of the logos on both of them. If that's the case, what does the normal D&R spine look like?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Apr 22, 2012 5:01 am futuramafreak wrote: Here is a picture: [url]http://img845.imageshack.us/img845/8370/img3306v.jpg[/url] I realized looking at this picture, comparing the Manga logos, that the purple one might just be completely washed out of red. It's a pretty drastic change, though. I just might not have seen that before because the front and back covers are identical. D&R and the first EoE came from the same place, and you can kinda see the washing-out of the logos on both of them. If that's the case, what does the normal D&R spine look like?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 22, 2012 9:28 am What Svenge said. It's cover fade. Sell the faded one for profit.

[]:             Quote Post by futuramafreak » Mon Apr 23, 2012 10:56 pm That was the plan, I'm glad you agree. The faded one was $16, and the nice one was $10. I'll leave it up to eBay to decide how much it's worth, but maybe start it at like $20? Thanks for the input!

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Apr 24, 2012 12:15 am futuramafreak wrote: That was the plan, I'm glad you agree. The faded one was $16, and the nice one was $10. I'll leave it up to eBay to decide how much it's worth, but maybe start it at like $20? Thanks for the input!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Apr 24, 2012 10:16 am Just make sure you indicate that the cover is faded, otherwise you might get a buyer who will neg or claim against you for not specifying it.
